## reference · Miller's Law and Chunking

* The average person can hold 7 (±2) items in their working memory.
* Organize content into smaller chunks to aid processing, understanding, and memorization.
* Short-term memory capacity varies per individual, influenced by prior knowledge and situational context.
* Avoid using the "magical number seven" to justify unnecessary design limitations.

Sources: https://lawsofux.com/millers-law/
